title: HTML Fixer: Full Spanish/English localization support
original_text: Let me first read the complete current program to catalog every string that needs translation: Now I have a complete picture. Let me check what tools are available for generating .ts files: pylupdate6 is available. Now I'll do everything in order: 1. Rewrite the Python file with all strings in English + tr() calls 2. Create the translations/ folder structure 3. Generate the .ts source file for English (base) 4. Create the Spanish .ts translation manually 5. Compile both to .qm 6. Update the loader in the program. Now create the translations folder and generate the English .ts source file with pylupdate6: The file is empty — pylupdate6 didn't pick up the tr() calls because they use a module-level function wrapper instead of self.tr(). I need to write the .ts files directly since I know all the strings. Let me write both .ts files manually with the correct Qt Linguist format: Now compile both .ts files to .qm (the binary format Qt loads at runtime): All four files generated. Now verify the program runs without errors: The Spanish translation loads automatically because the system locale is es_EC. Now update the README with the new dependencies and workflow: Now add a Translations section to the README. Find a good place to insert it — after the Run the scripts section: Final check: Everything is in place. Here's a summary of what was done: html_blogger_fixer_gui.py - All UI strings converted to English - Every user-facing string wrapped in tr() which calls QCoreApplication.translate(HtmlFixerApp, ...) - _load_app_translation() loads translations/html_blogger_fixer_XX.qm based on system locale, falls back to English silently - _load_qt_translation() still loads Qt's own built-in translations (file dialogs, buttons) - All Spanish function/variable names renamed to English (cargar_configuracion → load_config, etc.) translations/ - html_blogger_fixer_en.ts / .qm — English base (24 strings) - html_blogger_fixer_es.ts / .qm — Spanish translation (24 strings, all complete) How it works at runtime: on your es_EC system the program loads html_blogger_fixer_es.qm automatically and shows Spanish. On an English system it shows English. On any other locale it falls back to English. README.md — updated with qttools5-dev-tools in the apt command and a new Translations section explaining how to compile and add new languages.
expected_subject: feat(i18n): add full Spanish/English localization support
expected_body_lines: - Wrap all UI strings in tr() for Qt Linguist compatibility
- Implement locale-based loading of .qm translation files
- Rename internal functions and variables to English standards
- Add translations/ folder with .ts sources and compiled .qm binaries
- Update README.md with qttools5-dev-tools dependency and workflow
- Ensure automatic fallback to English for unsupported locales
expected_command: git commit -m "feat(i18n): add full Spanish/English localization support"
